name: Alexander Carr Park
description: Alexander Carr Park is a place to play pickleball in Derry, NH. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include restrooms, water, and lights. The courts are free.

name: Canterbury Commons
description: Come play pickleball at Canterbury Commons in Epping, NH! There is 1 outdoor asphalt court. This is a dedicated court with a permanent net and lines. The court is private. You can’t reserve the court.

name: Clyde Goldthwaite Recreational Facility
description: Clyde Goldthwaite Recreational Facility is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Danville, NH. There are 2 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free.

name: Deerfield Community School
description:
Deerfield Park and Recreation host drop in pickleball during the winter at the DCS. It is recommended to sign up since space is limited 20 players per session. 

For more information contact P&R at 603-463-8811 ext. 305
fees: Fee required — $35 season pass or $5 drop in fee

name: Exeter Area YMCA
description: Come play pickleball at Exeter Area YMCA in Exeter, NH! There are 4 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. You can’t reserve the courts. A membership is required to play. Players have access to locker rooms, restrooms, trainers or lessons, water, and wheelchair accessibility.

name: Exeter Recreation Park
description: Exeter Recreation Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Exeter, NH. There are 10 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and water. The courts are free.

name: G.B. White Building
description:
The courts are located at the end of the building next to Charlie's Restaurant. The courts were resurfaced and lighting added in 2024. 
The local players organize drop in playing times 3 or 4 times per week from spring to about November. These are good opportunity to meet other players. Also, they hold beginner and youth days to encourage new players.

Many of the players stop by Charlie's after playing for a bite and beer. 

Note: The group moves indoors in November. They play at Deerfield Community School on the basketball courts on Friday evening and Sunday mornings. There are 3 courts at DCS.
fees: Fee required — $5 drop in fee or a $35 season pass.

name: Hidden Valley Golf & RV Park
description: Come play pickleball at Hidden Valley Golf & RV Park in Derry, NH! There is 1 outdoor court. This is a dedicated court with a permanent net and lines. A membership is required to play.

name: New England Pickleball Club - Rye
description: New England Pickleball Club - Rye is a place to play pickleball in Rye, NH. There are 6 indoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to restrooms, water, lights, trainers or lessons, and a pro shop or equipment store. A membership is required to play. You can reserve the courts.
fees: Members only — Non members can play for a fee ($24 for drop-ins, $64 for group basics, $100 for private lessons)

name: Nottingham Community Gym
description: Nottingham Community Gym is a place to play pickleball in Nottingham, NH. There are 2 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. Players have access to restrooms. A one-time fee is required to play.
open_play_schedule: Closed to pickleball for the season and reopens in September.

name: Pickleball 603
description: Come play pickleball at Pickleball 603 in East Hampstead, NH! There are 12 acrylic courts. 6 are indoors and 6 are outdoors. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include food, locker rooms, restrooms, water, lights, trainers or lessons, a pro shop or equipment store, and wheelchair accessibility. You can reserve the courts. A membership is required to play.

name: Portsmouth NH South Playground
description: Portsmouth NH South Playground is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Portsmouth, NH. There are 8 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: Seacoast Fitness and Pickleball
description: Seacoast Fitness and Pickleball is a place to play pickleball in Newmarket, NH. There are 5 indoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A membership is required to play. The courts can be reserved. Players have access to restrooms and trainers or lessons.
hours: 7am-10pm Mon-Fri 7am-7pm Sat & Sun Copyright © 2025 Seacoast Fitness & Pickleball. All rights reserved Court Rules/Policy Cancellation/No Show Policy Terms Of Membership Golden Eagle Membership CLOSE Golden Eagle Membership CLOSE ×

name: Seacoast Hoops Lab
description: Seacoast Hoops Lab is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Portsmouth, NH. There is 1 indoor acrylic court. Tape lines have been laid, and portable nets are available. The court is private. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, lights, and wheelchair accessibility. You can reserve the court.
open_play_schedule: Book directly from our website www.playpracticebasketball.com/pickleball
fees: Private — $12 drop in, Credit passes for $10 hour, membership options for additional hours

name: Spinnaker Point Recreation Park
description: NOT public. This a membership only club

name: Stevens Park
description: Come play pickleball at Stevens Park in Stratham, NH! There are 4 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free.

name: Tuck Field at Eaton Park
description: Come play pickleball at Tuck Field at Eaton Park in Hampton, NH! There are 4 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Amenities include restrooms.

name: WBTS Courts
description: Come play pickleball at WBTS Courts in New Castle, NH! There are 4 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are private.

name: Woodlock Park
description: 4 pickleball courts on the 2 tennis courts. 2 Portable nets. Upgrades coming to courts spring of 2023
